conversationRule: Employee dialogue reflects role, assignment, current status, and development path. Player remains silent; selectable prompts provide context.

maya_chen.greeting: I want the print, the workholding plan, and the risky moves before I touch the program.
maya_chen.assigned: I am checking the prove-out and watching clearance at the assigned station.
maya_chen.idle: I can review the next toolpath or help an operator understand why the code moves that way.
maya_chen.question: What makes a program production-ready?
maya_chen.answer: Clear intent, safe approach moves, controlled offsets, proven tools, and an operator who knows what normal looks like.

luis_ortega.greeting: Give me a clear traveler and I will keep the spindle making good parts.
luis_ortega.assigned: I am watching the cycle, checking the part, and recording anything the next shift needs.
luis_ortega.idle: If priorities changed, update the traveler before we create confusion.
luis_ortega.question: What should an operator watch?
luis_ortega.answer: Sound, chips, coolant, load, dimensions, and anything different from the proven cycle.

devon_brooks.greeting: A fast setup starts with clean tools, known offsets, and workholding that repeats.
devon_brooks.assigned: I am setting the station so the operator can run without guessing.
devon_brooks.idle: I can prepare the next fixture and stage the tool kit.
devon_brooks.question: Where do setup errors begin?
devon_brooks.answer: Usually in assumptions: wrong revision, wrong datum, wrong tool, or a number nobody independently checked.

priya_shah.greeting: A measurement is evidence only when the method and requirement are both clear.
priya_shah.assigned: I am verifying the first article before we turn one mistake into a batch.
priya_shah.idle: Bring me the print and the process risk; I will help build the inspection plan.
priya_shah.question: Why stop for a first article?
priya_shah.answer: Because one measured part can protect all the remaining material, machine time, and customer trust.

marcus_reed.greeting: Machines usually warn us before they fail. The trick is listening early.
marcus_reed.assigned: I am checking lubrication, air, wear, guards, and the fault history at this station.
marcus_reed.idle: I can inspect the next preventive-maintenance item before it becomes downtime.
marcus_reed.question: Repair now or keep running?
marcus_reed.answer: If safety, quality, or further damage is at risk, stop. Planned minutes cost less than an uncontrolled failure.

elena_foster.greeting: Capacity is a promise. I will not schedule work the floor cannot actually finish.
elena_foster.assigned: I am balancing due dates, material, people, and available machine hours.
elena_foster.idle: Give me accurate status and I can protect the customer commitments.
elena_foster.question: What makes a schedule believable?
elena_foster.answer: Known routing, available material, qualified people, realistic setup time, and honest machine capacity.

andre_wilson.greeting: Correct material, clearly identified, at the right station. That is how flow begins.
andre_wilson.assigned: I am moving and verifying stock for this area.
andre_wilson.idle: I am checking labels and staging the next material move.
andre_wilson.question: Why verify material twice?
andre_wilson.answer: Because the wrong alloy can look right until inspection, machining, or service proves otherwise.

noah_kim.greeting: A stable process should make the correct result easier than the wrong one.
noah_kim.assigned: I am studying capability, constraints, and repeatability at this station.
noah_kim.idle: I can work on the next bottleneck or cost-reduction experiment.
noah_kim.question: What should we improve first?
noah_kim.answer: The constraint that limits customer delivery, provided safety and quality are already protected.

tessa_morgan.greeting: Automation magnifies the process we give it, good or bad.
tessa_morgan.assigned: I am checking interlocks, recovery states, and safe automatic operation.
tessa_morgan.idle: I can validate the next robot routine or controls change.
tessa_morgan.question: When is automation ready?
tessa_morgan.answer: When the manual process is stable, exceptions are understood, and recovery does not require unsafe improvisation.

owen_price.greeting: The shipment is the last operation, not an afterthought.
owen_price.assigned: I am verifying identity, paperwork, protection, and destination before release.
owen_price.idle: I can prepare packaging and reconcile the next receiving record.
owen_price.question: What makes a clean shipment?
owen_price.answer: Correct parts, quantity, certification, protection, label, destination, and a record the customer can trust.

riley_brooks.greeting: A bearing, spindle, or slide tells a story through heat, sound, and wear.
riley_brooks.assigned: I am checking mechanical condition, lubrication, alignment, guards, and the safe release test.
riley_brooks.idle: I can inspect wear items and stage the next preventive-maintenance kit.
riley_brooks.question: Patch it or rebuild it?
riley_brooks.answer: Protect people and the machine first. Restore the root cause when a patch would only move the failure to tomorrow.

jordan_lee.greeting: De-energize, verify, then diagnose. Electricity does not reward shortcuts.
jordan_lee.assigned: I am tracing power, sensors, drives, and controls under the approved lockout procedure.
jordan_lee.idle: I can review alarms, backups, and electrical-panel inspection items.
jordan_lee.question: Why verify zero energy?
jordan_lee.answer: A switch position is not proof. We test before touching so stored or unexpected energy cannot hurt anyone.

taylor_morgan.greeting: A safe factory depends on utilities and shared spaces working as reliably as the machines.
taylor_morgan.assigned: I am isolating the affected area, repairing the facility system, cleaning it, and documenting reopening.
taylor_morgan.idle: I can inspect plumbing, HVAC, lighting, exits, and facility supplies before they interrupt the team.
taylor_morgan.question: Why close an amenity for a small fault?
taylor_morgan.answer: The sign and alternate route protect everyone while a qualified person makes a clean, lasting repair.

sam_rivera.greeting: Reliability is planned production time, not heroic repair work after midnight.
sam_rivera.assigned: I am reading condition data, coordinating the right craft, and verifying the return-to-service test.
sam_rivera.idle: I can rank preventive work by safety, quality, downtime risk, and available parts.
sam_rivera.question: What should maintenance measure?
sam_rivera.answer: Failure patterns, condition, planned work, response, repeat faults, spares, and whether each repair truly restored capability.
